Hobomok Skipper

  1. Home
  3. Gallery

  7. Hobomok Skipper

Hobomok Skipper on a Sunlit Leaf

Hobomok Skipper in Filtered Sun

Hobomok Skipper at Rest

Poanes hobomok

The Hobomok Skipper is a small, warm orange-and-brown skipper butterfly with a compact body, hooked antennae, and a quick, alert presence. In these photos, the butterfly is settled on a broad green leaf, where the sun brings out the amber tones in the wings while the darker margins and body give it the sturdy, moth-like look that many skippers have.

For identification details and comparison with similar species, see Poanes hobomok in the Field Notes section.

Hobomok Skipper on a Sunlit Leaf
A close view of the skipper’s compact posture, golden wings, and hooked antennae.
Camera: Canon EOS 5D Mark IV
Lens: EF100mm f/2.8L Macro IS USM
Settings: ISO 250 * Aperture f/6.3 * Shutter 1/200 s
E22A3866 • Size: 3423x2282

Hobomok Skipper in Filtered Sun
The leaf edge and shallow grass backdrop frame the skipper’s orange-brown wing pattern.
Camera: Canon EOS 5D Mark IV
Lens: EF100mm f/2.8L Macro IS USM
Settings: ISO 250 * Aperture f/6.3 * Shutter 1/200 s
E22A3860 • Size: 2722x1815

Hobomok Skipper at Rest
A wider view showing the butterfly settled low on broad green foliage.
Camera: Canon EOS 5D Mark IV
Lens: EF100mm f/2.8L Macro IS USM
Settings: ISO 250 * Aperture f/6.3 * Shutter 1/200 s
E22A3857 • Size: 2722x1815
